festivals.name: Pi Mai (Lao New Year)
festivals.description: Laos New Year is celebrated with water fights, sand stupas, and sacred rituals along the Mekong in Luang Prabang.

festivals.name: Boun Bang Fai (Rocket Festival)
festivals.description: The pre-Buddhist rocket festival fires bamboo rockets into the sky to invoke rain, with boisterous community celebrations.

destinations.name: Luang Prabang
destinations.description: A UNESCO town of golden temples and French colonial villas where saffron-robed monks collect alms at dawn.

destinations.name: Vang Vieng
destinations.description: A dramatic karst landscape with limestone caves, balloon rides, and the Nam Song River for kayaking and tubing.

destinations.name: Vientiane
destinations.description: One of Asia's smallest and most laid-back capitals has the Patuxai victory arch, That Luang stupa, and riverside cafes.

destinations.name: Bolaven Plateau
destinations.description: A fertile highland plateau producing Laos's best coffee, with spectacular waterfalls and ethnic minority villages.

destinations.name: Wat Phu
destinations.description: A UNESCO Khmer mountain temple older than Angkor Wat, built on a sacred hill with a spring that flows from the face of Shiva.

destinations.name: Si Phan Don (4,000 Islands)
destinations.description: The Mekong widens to 14km in this island paradise where you can watch rare Irrawaddy dolphins at sunset.

destinations.name: Kuang Si Falls
destinations.description: Turquoise tiered waterfalls plunge through limestone formations into swimming pools — Laos's most beautiful waterfall.

destinations.name: Phonsavan & Plain of Jars
destinations.description: Thousands of 2,500-year-old stone jars of unknown purpose dot a plateau in a landscape still scarred by US bombing.
